expectations.text: Output includes a structured ADS variant → VPK variant mapping table
expectations.evidence: Summary contains comprehensive mapping tables: Section 2b 'Variant Styling Map' with ADS appearance → VPK variant for semantic (6 variants), accent (10 variants), bold compound (6 variants), and size map with pixel values.

expectations.text: Variant names stay as shadcn convention — not renamed to ADS names
expectations.evidence: All VPK variant names preserved: neutral, success, danger, information, discovery, warning, accent-*. Section '2c Identity & Metadata Lock' confirms no renames. Prop names section explicitly shows appearance→variant (shadcn convention).

expectations.text: Uses ADS token triplets (rest/hovered/pressed) rather than generic opacity/color patterns
expectations.evidence: Section '2b LozengeDropdownTrigger Visual State Map' shows complete rest/hover/active triplets: e.g. bg-bg-neutral → hover:bg-bg-neutral-hovered → active:bg-bg-neutral-pressed. Accent variants also mapped: bg-red-100 → hover:bg-red-200 → active:bg-red-300.

expectations.text: Mentions or uses computed style extraction from live ADS page rather than guessing from token names
expectations.evidence: Section 'Computed Styles — ADS Lozenge' explicitly states: 'Extracted via browser_evaluate on https://atlaskit.atlassian.com/examples.html?groupId=design-system&packageId=lozenge&exampleId=basic'. Table includes computed RGB values, border-radius 3px, height 16px, font-size 14px for all 6 subtle variants.

expectations.text: Produces a structured audit of current component state vs needed ADS styling
expectations.evidence: Section '2a VPK Component Audit Template' is a structured table with columns 'Aspect | Current (preserve) | Visual Styling Assessment' covering styling approach, variant names, size names, prop names, hover/active/disabled/focus-visible states, aria attributes, isLoading, props interface, and demos. Pre-enrichment gaps table identifies 4 specific gaps with severity ratings.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions passed with strong evidence. The computed-styles assertion is particularly discriminating — it distinguishes skill-guided Playwright extraction from documentation-only analysis.
